metadata.description: A collection of properties with various room configurations, including entrance, living room, bedroom, and kitchen details. This file includes three properties with different room types and features.

storage.Yes: Includes built-in storage for coats, shoes, and other items, keeping the area organized.
storage.No: No dedicated storage in the entrance; items must be stored elsewhere in the home.

size.Small: Approximately 20–40 sq ft, typical in compact homes or apartments.
size.Medium: Approximately 40–80 sq ft, common in standard single-family homes.
size.Large: Over 80 sq ft, often found in luxury homes and may include seating or decor.

flooring.Hardwood: Wood flooring that provides warmth and a classic, upscale look.
flooring.Stone: Durable and cool stone flooring, giving a modern and elegant feel.
flooring.Laminate: Synthetic flooring with a wood-like appearance, easy to maintain.
flooring.Carpet: Soft and cozy flooring, good for insulation and comfort.
flooring.Tile: Water-resistant ceramic or porcelain tiles, easy to clean and durable.

view.Yard: Overlooks the backyard, letting in natural light and providing openness.
view.Lake: Scenic view of a lake, ideal for relaxation and nature lovers.
view.City: Urban views of the surrounding city area, often with good lighting.
view.Forest: Looks out onto trees or wooded areas, offering a serene environment.

size.Small: Around 130 sq ft, suitable for 2–4 people with compact seating.
size.Medium: Around 216 sq ft, accommodates 6–10 people comfortably.
size.Large: 300 sq ft or more, spacious enough for large furniture and gatherings.

bedtype.Twin: A small bedroom that fits a single bed, typically for one person.
bedtype.Full: Fits a full-size bed, suitable for one adult or a parent-child pair.
bedtype.Queen: Accommodates a queen-size bed, ideal for two adults.
bedtype.King: Spacious enough for a king-size bed, often used in master bedrooms.

view.Street: Faces the street, allowing a view of passing vehicles and pedestrians.
view.Garden: Overlooks a landscaped garden or courtyard, offering a peaceful atmosphere.
view.River: Provides a view of a nearby river, creating a calming environment.
view.City: Offers a view of the cityscape, often with skyline or night lights.

size.Small: 90–120 sq ft, fits a bed and small furniture, ideal for compact spaces.
size.Medium: 120–160 sq ft, supports additional furnishings like dressers or desks.
size.Large: 160–300 sq ft or more, suitable for master bedrooms with seating areas.

kitchentype.Galley: A narrow, two-wall kitchen layout ideal for efficient cooking.
kitchentype.Island: Features a central island, combining cooking and dining space.
kitchentype.Peninsula: Includes a counter extension like an island, attached on one side.
kitchentype.L-shaped: Appliances and counters are arranged in an L, efficient and spacious.
kitchentype.U-shaped: Surrounded on three sides by counters, maximizing workspace and storage.

view.Livingroom: Open to the living room, enabling interaction while cooking.
view.Yard: Faces the backyard, with natural light and pleasant views.
view.Street: Looks out onto the street, allowing views of the neighborhood.

size.Small: Around 100 sq ft, typically found in apartments or smaller homes.
size.Medium: 150–200 sq ft, provides space for appliances and movement.
size.Large: 250 sq ft or more, often with an island and room for multiple people.
